title: Page-level content: data model + migration (tabs become optional)
description: Foundational data-model change so a page can hold its own body content, with tabs becoming an OPTIONAL extra. Today all content lives in tabs: `blocks.tab_id` is a NOT NULL FK to `tabs`, so a page with zero tabs has nowhere to store content. This task decides and lands the schema + migration only; the content pipeline (TASK-66), admin editor (TASK-67), and public render (TASK-68) build on it.
acceptanceCriteria: The chosen data model lets a page own body content without any user-created tab, while preserving tabs as optional additional views

A migration lands via `pnpm --filter @systembook/server db:generate` (never hand-edited) and backfills existing pages so no current content is lost ‚Äî existing tabs and their blocks stay intact and reachable
The primary/body container is distinguishable from user tabs at the data layer (a flag or reserved marker) so downstream tasks can hide it from the tab bar
Schema types and any runtime block/tab helpers compile (`pnpm typecheck`) and existing server tests still pass

steps.details: RECOMMENDED (lowest risk): every page owns one implicit 'primary' content container that renders as the page body without tab chrome; user tabs are additional. Implement by guaranteeing a system primary tab per page (keep `blocks.tab_id` NOT NULL) ‚Äî so the revisions/snapshot/FTS machinery that is keyed on tabs (`buildPageSnapshot`, `serialize.ts`, `reindexPageFts`) needs no structural change. ALTERNATIVE (larger blast radius): make `blocks.tab_id` nullable and add nullable `blocks.page_id` with a one-of constraint. Confirm the choice before writing the migration ‚Äî it determines the scope of TASK-66/67/68.

Apply the step-1 decision in `apps/server/src/db/schema.ts`. For the primary-tab approach: add a marker to `tabs` (e.g. `is_primary` boolean or a reserved `tipo`) so a page's body container is identifiable and excludable from the tab bar. Keep FK cascade semantics.

Run `db:generate` to create the migration. Add the backfill so every existing page gets a primary container: promote each page's first tab (lowest ordem) to primary, or create one where a page has no tabs. Ensure idempotency and FK integrity.

Run migrations on a fresh temp DB and on a copy with existing content; confirm no content is lost and every page has exactly one primary container. `pnpm --filter @systembook/server test` and `pnpm typecheck` green.

technicalNotes: This decision is the crux of the whole page-content feature: the primary-tab approach keeps the revisions/snapshot/FTS pipeline (keyed on tabs) intact and is strongly preferred for the MVP; the nullable page_id approach is conceptually cleaner but touches every consumer of blocks.

Migrations in `apps/server/drizzle/` are generated, never hand-edited ‚Äî the one documented exception is raw virtual tables (FTS5). A data backfill may need raw SQL appended in the generated migration; follow the FTS5 precedent if so.
